rules.label: Anthropomorphism and authenticity support reciprocity
rules.rationale: Aggregate Rule 1: anthropomorphism plus authenticity increases reciprocal interaction.

rules.label: Continuity strengthens reciprocity
rules.rationale: Aggregate Rule 2: high continuity supports stronger reciprocal interaction.

rules.label: Repeated one-to-one supportive AI contexts deepen disclosure
rules.rationale: Aggregate Rule 3: repeated one-to-one supportive interaction with anthropomorphism increases deeper self-disclosure.

rules.label: Companionship motives amplify deeper disclosure
rules.rationale: Aggregate Rule 4: companionship or social motivation moderates repeated interaction toward deeper disclosure and attachment.

rules.label: Low authenticity suppresses disclosure
rules.rationale: Aggregate Rule 5: scripted or low-authenticity AI behavior lowers trust and personal disclosure.

rules.label: High responsiveness increases disclosure
rules.rationale: Aggregate Rule 7 shows high responsiveness increases self-disclosure; later intimacy papers make that pathway relationally important.

rules.label: Low fear of judgment supports more and deeper disclosure
rules.rationale: Aggregate Rule 8: lower interpersonal fear of judgment increases disclosure, especially for sensitive topics.

rules.label: High information sensitivity reduces honesty
rules.rationale: Aggregate Rule 11: greater anthropomorphism or social presence in sensitive contexts can reduce truthful disclosure.

rules.label: Speaking or listening mode increases disclosure
rules.rationale: Aggregate Rule 13: speaking or listening modality yields more self-disclosure than typing or reading in many settings.

rules.label: Social relevance of a platform increases disclosure
rules.rationale: Aggregate Rule 15: higher perceived social relevance of the platform increases social-web self-disclosure.

rules.label: Restricted access supports deeper and more honest disclosure
rules.rationale: Aggregate Rule 17: restricted or specific-group access supports more sensitive disclosure than open public access.

rules.label: Large heterogeneous audiences increase posting but flatten depth
rules.rationale: Aggregate Rules 18 and 19 together suggest bigger heterogeneous audiences increase activity while pushing toward lowest-common-denominator presentation.

rules.label: Large public networks tilt expression positive
rules.rationale: Aggregate Rule 20: larger public networks produce more positive and less negative emotional presentation.

rules.label: Perceived disclosure by others increases own disclosure
rules.rationale: Aggregate Rule 21: higher perceived disclosure by others increases own social-media disclosure.

rules.label: Observed gains to others increase disclosure
rules.rationale: Aggregate Rule 22: observed gains to others raise perceived gains to self and therefore increase disclosure.

rules.label: Early dyadic text CMC increases direct questioning
rules.rationale: Aggregate Rule 27: dyadic CMC produces more direct questioning than face-to-face in get-acquainted interaction.

rules.label: Early dyadic text CMC increases disclosure frequency
rules.rationale: Aggregate Rule 29: early dyadic stranger CMC more often increases disclosure frequency than face-to-face.

rules.label: Established face-to-face friendship supports deeper disclosure
rules.rationale: Aggregate Rules 30 and 33 indicate that established friendship disclosure depth, breadth, and understanding more often favor face-to-face than online.

rules.label: Repeated continuity broadens and deepens disclosure
rules.rationale: Aggregate Rule 32: greater relationship duration increases breadth, depth, and understanding.

rules.label: IM-style repeated friendship interaction increases intimacy
rules.rationale: Aggregate Rule 35: greater instant-messaging use with friends is associated with greater verbal, affective, and social intimacy.

rules.label: Reduced-cue romantic technology lowers breadth and depth
rules.rationale: Aggregate Rule 39: in ongoing romantic relationships, reduced-cue communication technologies are associated with lower breadth and depth than face-to-face conversation.

rules.label: Relationship development softens reduced-cue breadth penalties
rules.rationale: Aggregate Rule 40: higher relationship development weakens the negative breadth association for text-based communication in ongoing relationships.

rules.label: Face-to-face zero-acquaintance interaction raises perceived responsiveness
rules.rationale: Aggregate Rule 42: in zero-acquaintance interaction, face-to-face produces higher perceived partner responsiveness than synchronous text-based CMC.

rules.label: Initial liking encourages disclosure
rules.rationale: Aggregate Rule 46: greater initial liking for a partner increases self-disclosure to that partner.

rules.label: Responsiveness, understanding, and dialogue support intimacy
rules.rationale: Aggregate Rules 50 and 51 show that dyadic disclosure and perceived partner responsiveness are central to intimacy in ongoing interaction.

rules.label: Public social-web access suppresses intimate disclosure
rules.rationale: Aggregate Rule 53: open public access is associated with less intimate or sensitive disclosure than restricted or specific-group access in social-web settings.

rules.label: Public high-risk broadcast disclosure can reduce liking
rules.rationale: Aggregate Rule 54: in public broadcast SNS settings, intimate disclosure can become socially costly and reduce attraction when reputational stakes and judgments of appropriateness are salient.

rules.label: Broadcast audience pressure makes disclosure more deliberate
rules.rationale: Aggregate Rule 55: when reputational stakes, impression management pressure, and permanence are all high, broadcast disclosure shifts toward more deliberate and less spontaneous self-presentation.
